title: 3 Countercyclical, Intrinsically Undervalued Dividend Aristocrats

title: From Snowball to Avalanche: Understanding Buffett's Approach to Compound Interest
text: "Compound interest is like being at the top of a very large hill with wet snow and starting with a snowball and getting it rolling downhill," Warren Buffett (Trades, Portfolio) once said at a Berkshire Hathaway ( BRK.A , Financial) ( BRK.B , Financial) shareholder meeting. He attributed the metaphor to Charlie Munger (Trades, Portfolio), but it aptly describes the power of compounding returns – a fundamental principle that has guided his investment strategy from the beginning.
